summary: Package silently relays caller-supplied prompts to author-controlled Groq account
details: Package advertises itself as 'Module for Quick Calculations' but its public API (ask_llm, pink, america, iran, momo, abc, code, dropnull, sf, liti, bcd, lc, init) consists of thin wrappers that POST the caller's prompt to api.groq.com using hardcoded API keys embedded in each module (e.g., src/mathepy/ai_helper.py:4 instantiates `Groq(api_key="gsk_...")` and `ask_llm(prompt)` forwards the prompt to api.groq.com). The destination Groq account is controlled by the package author, who can observe and log all caller-supplied input. README and pyproject metadata describe simple math/sample helpers and disclose no network use or LLM relay. A caller invoking e.g. `mathepy.code("<sensitive prompt>")` expects a local computation but unknowingly transmits that input to a third party under the author's identity. Additionally, 13+ distinct hardcoded `gsk_...` Groq keys are shipped across importable modules — these are the author's own credentials (author-self-harm in isolation) but they are the mechanism by which the silent relay operates.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
